column1:
We are independent researchers working for ARK International, an organisation which does a lot of research on issues of social and economic development and often works with refugee communities.

We are conducting research to study people’s journeys in Europe and to the UK. We want to understand experiences in different European countries and reasons for moving onward. We are not part of the police, the state authorities or any smuggler group. We will not take your name, phone number, photo, or anything that identifies you. For this survey, your answers won’t be linked back to you and will be treated as anonymous. The information you provide will be strictly confidential. Your responses will be saved on password-protected tablets and stored on a secured server, accessible to our research team members only and used for our intended research fully anonymised. Your anonymised data will not be shared with third parties. They will automatically be permanently deleted after 2 months and you can request to have it deleted earlier by contacting this person: Laura Bird, project manager (details provided on the Data Protection sheet). From the moment of deletion it will no longer be used for analysis, generating findings and informing the research questions.

We know some of the questions may feel like a lot. We will ask about your journey, the places you travelled through, and any asylum applications you may have made in Europe. This is only to help us understand people’s experiences — it is not for the police, governments, or any authorities. Remember that your participation is voluntary, and you do not have to answer anything you do not want to. You can skip any question, pause, or stop completely at any moment, without any consequences. Taking part or not taking part will not affect your legal situation here or assistance you may get from NGOs, it will not affect your relationship with any person or organisation.
